status_detail: Development of the German Hydrogen Core Network (H2-Kernnetz) by Gasunie and partners. ~9,000 km of converted gas pipelines. Connecting electrolysis hubs in Lingen, Hamburg, Ruhr, and North Sea coast. Enabling large-scale hydrogen transport from production centers to industrial consumers. Phase 1 capacity by 2027.

data_management.narrative_summary: The German Hydrogen Core Network (H2-Kernnetz), led by Gasunie, OGE, Nowega, and other TSOs, is the backbone infrastructure for Germany's hydrogen economy. Approximately 9,000 km of existing natural gas pipelines will be converted to hydrogen transport, connecting production hubs in Lingen (GA-PR-020), Hamburg (GA-PR-004), the Ruhr region, and North Sea import terminals with major industrial consumers. The network is essential for enabling the large-scale electrolysis projects under development, as it provides the offtake infrastructure for hydrogen transport. Phase 1 is targeted for 2027, with full build-out by 2032. This is not an electrolysis project itself but a critical enabling infrastructure.

power.grid_connection_detail: Infrastructure project — no direct power connection

offtake.offtaker_name: Industrial hydrogen consumers across Germany via pipeline network

sources.title: German H2-Kernnetz — hydrogen core network investment framework approved 2025
